_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Symptomatic mesenteric cysts requiring surgical excision
Painless abdominal swelling with characteristic physical signs (fluctuant swelling near umbilicus, moves freely at right angles to mesentery - Tillaux's sign, zone of resonance around cyst)
Recurrent attacks of abdominal pain with or without vomiting (due to temporary impaction of a food bolus or torsion of mesentery)
Acute abdominal catastrophe (due to torsion, rupture, haemorrhage into cyst, or infection)

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment indication for procedure?
Did USG/CT/MRI Abdomen report confirm the diagnosis?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Imaging report (USG/CT/MRI Abdomen) does not confirm the diagnosis or is not indicative of surgery.
Clinical notes are missing detailed history, signs, symptoms, or planned line of treatment and indication for procedure.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are missing or incomplete.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are not available.
Histopathology examination report was not submitted.
Discharge summary is missing or lacks follow-up advice at the time of discharge.

clinical_key_pointers: Cysts of the mesentery are benign lesions.
Typically, the cyst is located in the umbilical region which moves at right angles to the direction of mesentery.
These are congenital cysts, enterogenous or chylolymphatic.
MESENTERIC CYST-TYPES: Chylolymphatic cyst, Enterogenous cyst, Urogenital remnant, Teratomatous dermoid cyst.
surgical_options: Partial excision with marsupialization (if enucleation and resection is not possible)
Chylolymphatic cyst: Enucleation (without sacrificing bowel, due to separate blood supply)
Enterogenous cyst: Excision of cyst with bowel segment (both share the same blood supply)
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY.
